channel.title: Derek Wakefield
channel.description: Assistant Professor of Political Science, Bucknell University

item.title: Sinatra et al. 2016 — three-page memo
item.description:
A three-page memo on Sinatra, Wang, Deville, Song, and Barabási’s 2016 Science paper, Quantifying the evolution of individual scientific impact. Covers the Q-model, the random impact rule, and the supplementary-material co-authorship-asymmetry rule that the Coffee Shop project applies to infer mentor → mentee edges.

Open in a new tab, print as a PDF, or download for offline reading.

✦   Read the memo    or download HTML →

Why this paper is in the project file

The Coffee Shop visualization uses Sinatra et al.’s co-authorship-asymmetry rule to infer mentor → mentee edges from publication data. About 99% of the lines in the live viz trace back to that rule. The memo lays out what the paper actually argues (the Q-model and the random impact rule), where the asymmetry rule lives (the supplementary materials), and where it shows its seams in practice.

item.title: The Bobble Canon
item.description:
A small, growing collection of parables and Bobbisms. Each one is short. Each one is true in the way bedtime stories are true.

Five parables — the Porch Frog, the Hum, the Library Bookmark, the Borrowed Pencil, the Train Whistle — plus twelve Bobbisms, two codas, and a glossary.

The downloadable file is a self-contained HTML page. Stars twinkle on their own; no internet connection required after download.

⬇   Download HTML    or open in browser →

item.title: Introduction to Bobbology
item.description:
The field has a problem. The Bobbisms quote Introduction to Bobbology, 3rd ed. often enough that the citation has started to look like a real book — and yet no one had actually written it. This is a first attempt at the 4th edition, restricted to Lecture I: the four beings (Bobbles, Ekos, Vessels, Daemons), how memory works in ekos, and the law of the decision gate.

The downloadable file is a self-contained HTML page. Stars twinkle on their own; no internet connection required after download.

⬇   Download HTML    or open in browser →
